resources.string: Delete all periods except the first?
Please enter valid break time (0–999 min)
Break is greater/equal to attendance time
Start time is before end time of previous period
Note: Changes here affect subsequent periods. Press \"Calculate\" to update all results.

A new period is for interruptions within the same working day.

Example: You work 8:00–12:00, take a longer break, then continue 13:30–17:00. Enter the two parts in separate periods – the app adds them together.

Previous periods can be edited at any time. After pressing "Calculate", all subsequent periods are automatically recalculated.

You can close a single period using the × on the last tab. To delete an earlier period, all subsequent periods must be closed first.

For a new working day, please close the period tabs via the menu item "New working day".

Basic usage:
Enter start time, end time, break and target hours, then tap "Calculate". The app shows the achieved time and the balance (overtime/undertime).

Start time buttons:
• "now" – enter current time
• "last" – use last saved start time

End time buttons:
• "earliest" – calculates earliest possible end time
• "now" – enter current time

Multiple periods:
A period represents a continuous work session on the same day – e.g. if you leave work for an extended break in between. Use "+" to add another period; calculate the current one first. The working times of all periods are added together. You can delete the last period using the "×" button on its tab. Previous periods can be edited at any time – after pressing "Calculate", all subsequent periods are automatically recalculated.

Two jobs:
Use "Switch job" to switch between two independent time accounts. Rename jobs under "Rename jobs".

History:
Use "Save day" to save the result of the current working day. Today's date is pre-filled but can be changed. Under "History" you can view all saved entries. From there you can also export the history as a text file.

Time format:
Under "Choose time format" you can switch between standard format (HH:MM) and industrial format (decimal hours, e.g. 8.75 h). The setting applies per job.

Automatic break:
Under "Automatic break" you can configure an automatic break deduction based on attendance duration – up to 4 tiers, separately for each job. Manually entered breaks are deducted in addition.

Reminder:
Tap the alarm icon in the sidebar to set a reminder for the calculated end of shift. The icon turns green while an alarm is active. Tapping again cancels the alarm. A long press shows the time the alarm is set for.

Quick access (sidebar):
The sidebar on the right edge provides direct access to the most important functions: Save day, History, Delete all periods, Switch job and this Help.

Version 1.19

© 2026 Michael Formanski
Leverkusen, Germany
Open Source (MIT License)

A simple flextime calculator.

Privacy Policy:
https://leverbursche.github.io/meine-gleitzeit-datenschutz/

Material Design Icons by Google are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License
Please calculate the last period first.
Please calculate the currently modified period first.
New work session on the same day.
Automatic break deduction by attendance duration, up to 4 tiers configurable.
The default 360 min → 30 min, 540 min → 15 min, min. break duration 15 min, is based on the German Working Hours Act, as of 2026.
The configured break time is always fully deducted.
Only the minutes exceeding the threshold are deducted.
Multiple tiers are cumulative: if more than one tier applies, all break times are added together. (E.g.: 360→30 and 540→15: with over 9 h attendance, 45 min are deducted.)

Important: If you open multiple time periods, gaps between them are only counted as breaks if they last at least this many minutes (empty = all gaps counted). Manually entered breaks are always deducted in addition. The automatic break deduction always occurs in the last time period tab.
Set reminder at end of shift
Please allow exact alarms in the app settings.
